name: DropdownColumns
description: Column customization panel adjacent to the main DropdownOptions. Drag-and-drop reorder, show/hide via Shown/Hidden sections, pin toggle, and staged Apply / Cancel. Uses pointer events (not HTML5 native drag) for reliable cross-browser, sandboxed-iframe, and nested-button behavior.

props.name: shown
props.description: Columns currently shown. ColumnItem: { id, label, pinned?, locked? }. Order in the array drives render order. locked=true: row is fixed (no drag, no hide, no pin toggle, Lock icon replaces GripVertical).

props.name: hidden
props.description: Columns currently hidden. Same ColumnItem shape; locked items here are non-interactive (no drag, no click-to-show).

states.rationale: The panel surface itself is passive. Composed ListItem rows use overlay hover. Dragging row drops to half opacity for visual feedback; drag-over target uses info-subtle fill. Pin toggle and Apply/Cancel Buttons carry their own state contracts via their Button variants.

keyboard.Tab: Move focus to the next focusable element — rows are tabbable (tabIndex=0) when not locked; nested Pin Buttons and footer Apply/Cancel are also in tab order
keyboard.Shift+Tab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
keyboard.Alt+ArrowUp: Move the focused row one position up within its section (Shown or Hidden). Locked rows in the path are skipped; the row jumps over them.
keyboard.Alt+ArrowDown: Move the focused row one position down within its section (same locked-skip rules).
keyboard.Enter: On a focused Hidden row, show the column (moves it to the end of the Shown list). On a focused Pin / Apply / Cancel Button: native button activation.
keyboard.Space: Same as Enter on Hidden rows and native buttons.
keyboard.Delete: On a focused Shown row, hide the column (moves it to the end of the Hidden list). Locked columns are immune.

accessibility.focusManagement: Each row is a non-interactive <div> (ListItem interactive=false) so the nested Pin <button> is the focusable child without nesting buttons. Rows are tabbable via tabIndex=0 (not set on locked rows). After any keyboard reorder, focus follows the row to its new index via requestAnimationFrame so consecutive Alt+Arrow presses keep working. Row :focus-visible draws a 2px box-shadow ring via --dls-state-focus-ring-color.

propertyMapping.note: Figma 'draggable' variant is a visual hint of the drag-handle / cursor. In code, all rows render the GripVertical handle and respond to pointer-down. The type variant is informational only.
Array of ColumnItem { id, label, pinned? }. Order in the array drives the rendered order.
Pin state is part of the ColumnItem shape. Toggling fires onChange with the new shown[] (pinned flag flipped). Icon: lucide Pin / PinOff. Pin Button is omitted for locked columns.

Driven by ColumnItem.locked. When true: replaces GripVertical handle with lucide Lock icon, disables drag (both as source and target via [data-locked] / :not([data-locked]) selectors), omits Pin Button, and adds aria-label="{label} (locked)".
Filled Button in the footer ListItem. Receives the staged (shown, hidden) payload.
Outline Button in the footer ListItem.

useWhen: Below the 'Columns' item in the DropdownOptions menu on a table top bar
Anywhere a user needs to choose which columns to show + pin in a data table
doNotUseFor: Column-level actions (rename, hide, pin from header) → use DropdownColumnActions
Selection / row-expansion utility columns — those control table behavior, not data display, and should not appear in this dropdown

knownDeviations.description: Spec mentions --dls-color-component-dropdown-columns-* tokens but they do not exist in tokens.json. CSS uses semantic surface/border/state tokens directly.
The Figma type=draggable variant is informational; in code all non-locked rows are draggable. The variant exists in Figma to document the drag affordance visually.
